text: Fill in the blanks — 3rd to 8th Week of Development
questions: The *primitive streak* appears on Day *14/14-15* in the caudal midline, establishing the body axes of the embryo.
Gastrulation converts the bilaminar disc into a *trilaminar/three-layered* disc consisting of ectoderm, *mesoderm*, and endoderm.
The organiser of the entire embryo is the *primitive node/Hensen's node:cranial end of primitive streak*, located at the cranial end of the primitive streak.
If the primitive streak fails to regress, remnant cells in the sacrococcygeal region can form a *teratoma/sacrococcygeal teratoma:tumour with all three germ layers*.
The *notochord* induces the overlying ectoderm to form the *neural plate*, which is the first step of neurulation.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the material and try again.
Good effort! Review the sections you missed.
